mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or out-of-sample predictive accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in existing home sales and new housing starts indicate a collapsing housing demand cycle, which reduces construction spending and consumer wealth, producing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with a bivariate regression coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 24-month sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle recovery periods, with a mean forward return difference of less than 0.1% compared to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates above 0.4% monthly while the federal funds rate remains unchanged, real rates turn negative and markets price in aggressive future tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of high CPIAUCSL growth and stable FEDFUNDS yields positive or flat 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered cases, invalidating the tightening fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 200,000 signal robust economic momentum and rising corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by > 200,000 in a single month
failure_condition: Months with PAYEMS gains > 200k show negative median 20-day forward returns, or the signal information coefficient drops below -0.01 over a rolling 36-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic expansion that supports corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, with a t-statistic below 1.5 for the slope coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Aggressive federal funds rate hikes combined with decelerating industrial production signal a policy-induced economic slowdown, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% in a single meeting
failure_condition: Large FEDFUNDS increases during periods of falling INDPRO fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 45% over a 24-month period.
